content:
# Conditional Statements

### Block of Code

A sequence of instructions are called block of code.  
Python executes code in a sequence.

### Condition
An expression that results in either `True` or `False`
  
*Examples* 

i. 2 < 3  
ii. a == b  
iii. True

## Conditional Statement

Conditional Statement allows you to execute a block of code only when a specific condition is `True` 

![](https://res.cloudinary.com/learning-platform/image/fetch/dpr_auto,w_auto,f_auto,q_auto/https://nkb-backend-ccbp-media-static.s3.ap-south-1.amazonaws.com/prod/media/profile_pic/card-notes-attachment/68befdc2-4720-4f82-aa0f-c5659e3e3810.PNG)

## Conditional Block
Block of code which executes only if a condition is `True` is called **Conditional Block**.

![](https://res.cloudinary.com/learning-platform/image/fetch/dpr_auto,w_auto,f_auto,q_auto/https://nkb-backend-ccbp-media-static.s3.ap-south-1.amazonaws.com/prod/media/profile_pic/card-notes-attachment/f4a247d2-392b-41d6-b8a6-790f9332bdd5.PNG)

### Indentation

- Space(s) in front of the conditional block is called *indentation*.
- Indentation(spacing) is used to identify Conditional Block.  
- Standard practice is to use *four spaces* for indentation.

![](https://res.cloudinary.com/learning-platform/image/fetch/dpr_auto,w_auto,f_auto,q_auto/https://nkb-backend-ccbp-media-static.s3.ap-south-1.amazonaws.com/prod/media/profile_pic/card-notes-attachment/eb3959dc-719c-4a7d-b9a6-07279f419bfe.PNG)


### Possible Mistakes

Each statement inside a conditional block should have the same indentation (spacing).

#### Wrong Code

```python|UGLY|ITALIC 
if True:
    print("If Block")
            print("Inside If")
```

#### Output

```

IndentationError: unexpected indent
```

#### Correct Code

```python
if True:
    print("If Block")
    print("Inside If")
```

## If - Else Syntax

When If - Else conditional statement is used, Else block of code executes if the condition is `False`

![](https://res.cloudinary.com/learning-platform/image/fetch/dpr_auto,w_auto,f_auto,q_auto/https://nkb-backend-ccbp-media-static.s3.ap-south-1.amazonaws.com/prod/media/profile_pic/card-notes-attachment/70fbdab6-038e-4b6e-9eb1-a95f1182916b.PNG)

### Using If-Else

#### Code
  
```python  
a = int(input())
if a > 0:  
    print("Positive")  
else:  
    print("Not Positive")  
print("End")  
```
  
#### Input
  
```

2  
```
  
#### Output  
  
```
Positive  
End
```

### Possible Mistakes in If-Else

Else can only be used along with if condition. It is written below if conditional block  
  
#### Code
  
```python
if False:
    print("If Block")  
print("After If")  
else:  
    print("Else Block")  
print("After Else")  
```


#### Output  
  
```

SyntaxError: invalid syntax  
```
  

<SingleLineWarning>
Note: No code is allowed in between if conditional block and else statement.
</SingleLineWarning>
